data.title: Regeneron reports positive results for two anti-coagulant drugs
data.description:
Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) reported positive Phase 2 data for its thrombosis drug candidates REGN7508 and REGN9933 and plans to initiate Phase 3 testing next year.

The company said REGN7508 is designed to maximize anticoagulant activity while minimizing bleeding risk. REGN9933 is aimed at providing an option for patients with the highest bleeding risk who are not candidates for currently available anticoagulants.

The Phase 2 studies evaluated REGN7508 and REGN9933 in the prevention of asymptomatic or symptomatic venous thromboembolism, or VTE, after unilateral total knee arthroplasty.

Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) said a pooled analysis showed REGN7508 was superior to enoxaparin and non-inferior to apixaban, and REGN9933 was non-inferior to enoxaparin, according to a statement.

Pfizer (PFE) and Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) market apixaban under the brand name Eliquis, while Sanofi (SNY) sells enoxaparin under the name Lovenox. The drugs are also available generically.

Regeneron (REGN) added there was no major bleeding or clinically relevant non-major bleeding in any arm of the studies. Likewise, no treatment-related serious adverse events were reported.

The company plans to begin Phase 3 testing of the drugs in 2025.

data.title: EU new car registrations fall 1.9% in November Y/Y
data.description:
In November 2024, new car registrations in the European Union decreased 1.9% Y/Y to 869,816 units, compared to a 1.1% rise in October 2024, the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association showed.

So far in 2024, new car registrations remained stable (+0.4%) and almost reached 9.7 million units.

Negative results were seen across two of the region’s four major markets. Registrations fell in France (-12.7%) and Italy (-10.8%), while Germany was slightly stagnated (0.5%). On the contrary, Spanish market continued growth for the third consecutive month, rising 6.4% in November.

Registrations for battery electric vehicles declined by 9.5% to 130,757 units, with their market share falling to 15%, down from 16.3% last year. YTD market volume for new battery-electric cars was 5.4% lower than the same period last year, with the total market share now at 13.4%. The slump was primarily driven by decreases in registrations in Germany (-21.8%) and France (-24.4%).

Plug-in hybrid car registrations for November also declined by 8.8%.

Hybrid-electric registrations grew in November, rising by 18.5%. The market share now stands at 33.2%, up from 27.5% in November 2023, surpassing petrol car registrations for the third consecutive month.

Petrol car sales dropped by 7.8% and now represent 30.6% of the market, down from 32.5% the prior year. The diesel car market saw a decline of 15.3%, resulting in a 10.6% share.

For the month of November, market share leaders in Europe by registrations are Volkswagen Group (OTCPK:VLKAF) at 27.8%, Stellantis (STLA) at 14.3%, Renault Group (OTCPK:RNSDF) at 11.7%, Toyota Motor (NYSE:TM) at 8.40%, Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF) at 7.3%, BMW (BMWYY) at 7.2%, Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF) at 6.0%, Ford Motor (NYSE:F) at 2.8%, Volvo Cars (OTCPK:VLVLY) at 2.7%, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) at 2.2%, Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) at 1.6%, Suzuki (OTCPK:SZKMY) at 1.5%, SAIC Motor at 1.4%, and Mazda (OTCPK:MZDAY) at 1.2%.

The below pie chart depicts the company and its market share in November.

data.title: BofA cuts rating on Chinese internet firm Youdao to 'neutral'
data.description:
Bank of America downgraded their investment rating on Chinese internet company Youdao (NYSE:DAO) to "neutral" from "buy."

The research firm thinks the recent rally in share price is attributable to investor interest in the company's decent ad growth, which was driven by expansion into new verticals, strong RTA revenue growth, and application of AI technologies.

"We applaud Youdao's progress on ads business and margin improvement, but most positives could be largely priced in after recent strong rally, in our view; hence we downgrade the stock... We think both ads and education sectors could be reshaped by AI technologies over LT, though ST benefits could be insignificant," BofA said on Thursday.

BofA said while the smart device growth for the company could remain healthy ahead with the roll-out of new products, they view learning services momentum to be likely muted amid more prudent marketing spending.

They also expect growth to slow in Q4 with tough comps and less demand from e-commerce ads during the "Double 11" shopping festival this year.

DAO has a PT of $8.10, hiked from $7, implying an upside of over 11%. Shares are up 77.7% so far this year, while the benchmark S&P index rose 23%.

data.title: ALX Oncology falls as Jefferies cuts on lead cancer drug
data.description:
ALX Oncology (NASDAQ:ALXO) traded lower on Thursday after Jefferies downgraded the cancer drug developer to Hold from Buy, noting that complete Phase 2 data from its lead candidate, evorpacept, has failed to meet expectations.

The downgrade comes as ALX (ALX) is scheduled to report updated data from its mid-stage ASPEN-06 trial for evorpacept at the 2025 ASCO meeting in January. The ongoing trial tests the experimental checkpoint inhibitor in HER2-positive gastric/gastroesophageal junction cancer.

While ALXO showed its proof-of-concept against solid tumors with favorable mid-stage data in 2023, “full Phase II data in gastric failed to meet expectations, and the efficacy had come way down in H2 of the study,” analyst Michael Yee wrote.

Slashing his price target on ALXO to $2 from $12, Yee noted mounting uncertainty over the company’s 2025 readouts and its challenges in showing incremental value for the CD47-targeting molecule.

“At this time, the burden is on ALXO to put up data and prove the additive value of evorpacept CD47 on top of SOC, which are already proven to work,” he wrote.

data.title: Nvidia, Super Micro Computer asked by Commerce Dept. on China chip issue: report
data.description:
• The U.S. Commerce Department has sent inquiries to Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) to find out how the former's chips ended up in China over the past year, The Information reported.
  • Nvidia shares rose on Thursday, rebounding from a recent sell-off in the Jensen Huang-led company. Shares of Dell and Super Micro were lower by roughly 1% and 2%, respectively.
  • The Commerce Department has also sent inquiries to other Nvidia partners, such as Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL), the news outlet added.
  • Five people were involved in smuggling Nvidia chips, despite recent inspections from Super Micro, the news outlet reported. Some of the chips have even had their serial numbers duplicated or the serial numbers of the servers were altered, the news outlet added.
  • The U.S. has had a long-standing export control on Nvidia's most powerful GPUs to China amid national security concerns.
  • Nvidia, Dell and Super Micro did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Avangrid edges higher as New York PSC approves sale to Spain's Iberdrola
data.description:
Avangrid (NYSE:AGR) ticked higher by 0.6% as the New York Public Service Commission approved its planned sale to Spain's Iberdrola (OTCPK:IBDSF).

The New York PSC approved the deal at a meeting on Thursday, according to a webcast of the event.

Spanish utility Iberdrola in May reached an agreement to purchase the remaining stake in Avangrid (AGR) it doesn't already own for about $2.55 billion. Iberdrola will acquire the 18.4% stake in Avangrid it doesn't already own for $35.75 a share.

data.title: UBS’s global equity focus list: stocks within health technology
data.description:
UBS analysts selected their top stocks from four technology subsectors – the following are from the “health-tech” group.

“Many of [technology’s] subsectors are seen as a leading indicator of innovation and societal progress today, such as mobile tech’s role in helping emerging nations leapfrog the PC era, the rise of electric vehicles as a better environmental choice, and cloud tech that helps enterprises process big data efficiently,” said Hartmut Issel, head of Equity and Credit APAC and CIO at UBS.

Health technology is one of the leading disruptors undergoing technological transformation and is expected to create consequential and enduring impact.

“While health-tech has faced a challenging past 18 months, its core drivers still appear intact,” said Issel. “Healthcare (XLF) spend as a percentage of global GDP is close to 11%, but represents only 5% of technology spend.”

He said there is a significant catch-up potential, with generative AI acting as a catalyst.

“We like companies that provide AI/ML-driven services in drug development, diagnostic companies with access to proprietary datasets, and medical device makers which can better incorporate data analysis in their products.”

The following stocks are attractive opportunities in the short- to medium-term. These are companies that would benefit from fiscal spending and tech innovations.

“We see industry leaders for 2030 emerging from these sectors,” said Issel. The following stocks are expected “to deliver superior earnings growth to the market, boosted by positive, durable structural trends supporting the theme.”

UBS also named the leading stocks in green tech, enabling tech, and fin-tech.

Health-tech stocks:

  • Alcon (ALC) – “Alcon deserves a P/E valuation premium relative to the overall market, given its attractive and improving margin as well as its fast profit growth.”
  • Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) – “Intuitive’s structural growth story…offers ample room to drive increased penetration of robotic-assisted surgery,” analysts said. “We think the company is in the early stages of building a fully integrated care delivery model that includes surgeon training, diagnostics, intervention, and data analytics.”
  • IQVIA Holdings (IQV) – “We expect IQVIA's robust backlog and tech/data-forward offerings to drive market share gains.”
  • Lonza Group AG (OTCPK:LZAGY) – “We expect Lonza’s growth over the medium term to be driven by its multi-year investment plans in biologics, a segment where the industry growth outlook looks promising.”
  • Stryker Corp. (SYK) – “Stryker's guidance for 2024 projects high single digit sales growth, and we expect continued healthy volumes to drive margin improvement and earnings upgrades.”

data.title: Ultragenyx submits BLA for gene therapy for Sanfilippo syndrome
data.description:
• Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (NASDAQ:RARE) has submitted a Biologics License Application to the U.S. FDA asking for accelerated approval for UX111 (ABO-102), an AAV gene therapy for Sanfilippo syndrome type A (MPS IIIA).
  • The submission is supported by data from a including from an ongoing that has shown UX111 has resulted in quick and sustained decreased levels of cerebral spinal fluid ("CSF") heparan sulfate ("HS"), and that sustained reduction in CSF HS over time was associated with improved long-term cognitive development.
  • The FDA has already agreed with Ultragenyx that CSF HS can be used as a surrogate endpoint for accelerated approval.

data.title: UBS’s global equity focus list: stocks within financial technology
data.description:
UBS analysts selected their top stocks from four technology subsectors – the following are from the “fin-tech” group.

“Many of [technology’s] subsectors are seen as a leading indicator of innovation and societal progress today, such as mobile tech’s role in helping emerging nations leapfrog the PC era, the rise of electric vehicles as a better environmental choice, and cloud tech that helps enterprises process big data efficiently,” said Hartmut Issel, head of Equity and Credit APAC and CIO at UBS.

Financial technology, or fin-tech, is one of the leading disruptors undergoing technological transformation and is expected to create consequential and enduring impact.

“Structural drivers such as rapid urbanization, digitalization trends around mobility, cloud, analytics, social media, emerging technologies, and stronger demand from millennials are supportive factors for continued fintech growth,” said Issel. “We expect fin-tech revenues to grow at a CAGR of 11.5% – from $285B in 2022 to $680B in 2030.”

The following stocks are attractive opportunities in the short- to medium-term. These are companies that would benefit from fiscal spending and tech innovations.

“We see industry leaders for 2030 emerging from these sectors,” said Issel. The following stocks are expected “to deliver superior earnings growth to the market, boosted by positive, durable structural trends supporting the theme.”

UBS also named the leading stocks in green tech, enabling tech, and health tech.

Fin-tech stocks:

  • Bank Mandiri (OTCPK:PPERF) – “Indonesian banks, such as Bank Mandiri, are expected to benefit from the U.S. and domestic rate-cut cycle, which will help lower bond yields and the cost of funds, thereby improving net interest margins.”
  • Bank of America (BAC) – “We consider Bank of America to be a well-managed, diversified universal bank and relatively well positioned to rebound from recent macroeconomic and pressures related to high interest rates.”
  • Barclays (BCS) – “[Its] management recently highlighted it is working on revised financial targets and is committed to returning capital to shareholders,” analysts said. “Trading at the low end of the European banking sector, we do not think this is reflected in today's valuation.”
  • Goldman Sachs Group (GS) – The bank “should benefit from rate cuts in major economies…its valuation is attractive, based on a 2025 price-earnings multiple.”
  • IntercontinentalExchange (ICE) – “The company can benefit from increased market volatility and trading volumes, which we think could remain elevated in this uncertain macro environment.”
  • Intesa SanPaolo (OTCPK:ISNPY) – “We like Intesa’s diversified revenue mix, especially in a year when interest rates are declining, and fee growth is expected to continue to pick up.”
  • MasterCard (MA) – “We think the next decade will reward investments in disruptive technology… [which should be] accelerated by the global rollout of 5G technology.”
  • Societe Generale (OTCPK:SCGLF) – This “is one of the cheapest stocks in the sector (trading below 0.4x book value),” analysts said. “With rebased market expectations following last year's investor day and self-help optionality, we think the stock looks attractive.”
  • Standard Chartered (OTCPK:SCBFF) – The bank trades below the sector-average. “We think this is unjustified for a bank that is on a path to 12% return on tangible equity with the potential to buy back shares well above the >$5B target.”
  • Vonovia (OTCPK:VONOY) – “We believe that declining interest rates could serve as a catalyst for the share price, as this would alleviate the pressure from expectations of higher future financing costs.”

data.title: UBS’s global equity focus list: stocks within ‘enabling technology’
data.description:
UBS analysts selected their top stocks from four technology subsectors – the following are from the “enabling” tech group.

“Many of [technology’s] subsectors are seen as a leading indicator of innovation and societal progress today, such as mobile tech’s role in helping emerging nations leapfrog the PC era, the rise of electric vehicles as a better environmental choice, and cloud tech that helps enterprises process big data efficiently,” said Hartmut Issel, head of Equity and Credit APAC and CIO at UBS.

Enabling technology is one of the leading disruptors undergoing technological transformation and is expected to create consequential and enduring impact.

“Major technologies like AI and cybersecurity should see swifter adoption from enterprises and governments alike,” said Issel. “While the digitalization across many sectors was accelerated by the pandemic, the larger reality is that these structural shifts have been underway for some time now, driven by continued investments in infrastructure.”

In addition, UBS analysts expect investors to benefit from higher tech spending that should result in stronger revenue growth for the upcoming years. The enabling tech industry is expected to growth to $1.2T by 2025.

The following stocks are attractive opportunities in the short- to medium-term. These are companies that would benefit from fiscal spending and tech innovations.

“We see industry leaders for 2030 emerging from these sectors,” said Issel. The following stocks are expected “to deliver superior earnings growth to the market, boosted by positive, durable structural trends supporting the theme.”

UBS also named the leading stocks in green tech, fin-tech, and health tech.

Enabling tech stocks:

  • Alibaba Group (BABA) – The company benefits from structural growth in China's e-commerce industry, “and we see its investment bearing fruit with the resumption of GMV (gross merchandise value) growth and the closing of the gap with its industry peers.”
  • CrowdStrike (CRWD) – “We favor CrowdStrike due to its consistent revenue growth, rapidly expanding partner ecosystem, and proven track record of integrating AI tools into its product offerings.”
  • Meta Platforms (META) – “We believe Meta will benefit from continued market share gains in digital advertising across its platforms and see significant upside potential related to its efforts in video and shops.”
  • Microsoft (MSFT) – “The integration [of AI into its products] would kick off with its browser ChatGPT enabled Bing and this could help in market share gains.”
  • Oracle Corp. (ORCL) – “The company is optimistic that its AI led GPU-centric commitment will contribute to growth from fiscal year 2025.”
  • Salesforce.com (CRM) – “We believe Salesforce is likely more insulated from a slowdown compared to its peers, given the company's high mix of recurring revenue and activist involvement aimed at expanding margins.”
  • ServiceNow (NOW) – The company “is well positioned to capture the growing demand for digital transformation services thanks to its strong competitive product ecosystem.”
  • Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) – “The company is well positioned to tap into the secular rise in the internet of things and artificial intelligence.”
  • Uber Technologies (UBER) – “The group has resilient gross bookings and strong margins and continues to pursue a subscription model to improve its earnings visibility.”
